sourceScope.sourceTitle: Model Governance: Some Considerations for Practicing Life Actuaries
sourceScope.sourceReference: American Academy of Actuaries practice note, April 2017
sourceScope.sourceStatusNote: Companion guidance / implementation guidance; non-binding and not authoritative regulatory text.

observedSectionWindows.note: Front matter, introduction, model, and model-development opener.
Governance policy, model risk, controls, and validation.
Validation-governance relationship, documentation, references, and definitions.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single practice note file.
Do not widen into the other Practice Notes files in the same folder.
Keep VM-20, VM-21, VM-22, VM-A, VM-C, VM-G, VM-M, VM-30, and VM-31 references review-only unless the same batch intentionally includes the referenced operational text.
Keep ASOP references review-only unless the same batch captures the quoted operational language.
Keep the disclaimer and the non-binding status visible as companion-guidance context, not as authoritative regulatory text.
exclusions: No full-document processing outside this source file.
No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other Practice Notes files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Front matter, introduction, model, and model development opener
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the front matter and opening model-development language together and stop before the governance sections begin.

topicMap.label: Model governance policy, model risk, governance, controls, and validation
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the governance, controls, and validation guidance together and stop before the documentation closeout.

topicMap.label: Validation-governance relationship, documentation, references, and definitions
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the validation and documentation closeout separate from any later practice note file.

proposedBatches.title: Model governance front matter and introduction slice

paragraphTarget.notes: Capture the front matter, disclaimer, introduction, model definition, and early model-development material as one narrow review-only slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the disclaimer, introduction, and early model-development material before the governance/control sections begin.

proposedBatches.title: Model governance, controls, and validation slice

paragraphTarget.notes: Capture model governance policy, model risk, controls, and validation as one narrow review-only slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the governance and control guidance together as a single companion-guidance slice.

proposedBatches.title: Validation, documentation, references, and definitions slice

paragraphTarget.notes: Capture the validation-governance relationship, documentation guidance, references, and definitions as the closing review-only slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the closing validation and documentation material while keeping the end boundary explicit.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat an item as regulatory text only when the practice note explicitly quotes a requirement and the excerpt clearly directs action.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat model, role, validation, and governance terms as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.governanceOrControlExpectation: Treat role assignment, access controls, change controls, and validation structure as governance or control expectations when they appear.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat documentation audience, recordkeeping, and validation-report guidance as documentation expectations when they appear.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to ASOPs, VM-20, VM-30, VM-31, and other practice notes as cross-reference context unless operationalized in the same batch.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat title pages, TOC entries, and explanatory prose as background unless they impose a requirement.
reviewStandards.caveatOrCompanionGuidance: Treat the non-binding disclaimer and companion-guidance posture as caveat material, not as authoritative regulation.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat each page window as review-only and do not widen it unless later cleanup explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat model-risk ranking, validation frequency, and documentation audience choices as requiring human review unless the source text is explicit.

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The model-governance practice-note plan markdown must exist and carry the expected headings.
Every planned batch must remain review-only by default.
The source posture must stay explicitly active, with the companion-guidance note called out.
Each planned batch must have a matching batch definition.
The model-governance practice-note batch-definition file must stay synchronized with the plan.
Page locators should remain visible in the review packet.
futureChecksSuggested: Keep the plan portable if later practice notes need to be split into additional waves.
Keep the review-only guardrails visible until the review index and self-review note are created.

operatingNote.citationGuidance: Page locators are the primary anchor and line references are expected where the runner preserves them.
operatingNote.boundaryGuidance: Keep the non-binding disclaimer visible as a review-only caveat rather than a promotion signal.
operatingNote.appendixGuidance: Keep the governance, validation, documentation, and definitions closeout separate so the source ends at a visible boundary.
